name: Snowflake
tagline: SAS wins on advanced analytics, industry solutions, and production ML governance beyond data warehousing

overview.whatTheyDo: Cloud data platform specializing in data warehousing, data sharing, and data engineering. Expanding into ML with Snowpark (Python/Java/Scala in Snowflake), Cortex AI (LLMs), and Snowflake ML (model development). Marketplace for data sharing.
overview.sweetSpot: Organizations needing scalable cloud data warehouse with separation of compute and storage. Data sharing across organizations. SQL-centric analytics teams wanting to add ML without leaving Snowflake.
overview.typicalBuyer: CDO, VP Data Engineering, Head of Data Platform, VP Analytics
overview.phPresence: Limited direct PH presence but growing through ASEAN expansion from Singapore. Some PH enterprises evaluating for cloud data warehouse. Available through cloud marketplaces.
overview.recentMomentum: Snowpark (compute in Snowflake), Cortex AI (GenAI/LLM), Snowflake ML, Arctic (open-source LLM), Native App Framework. Pivoting from pure data warehouse to 'Data Cloud' platform.
overview.pricingModel: Consumption-based (credits per second of compute). Storage separate from compute. Transparent pricing but costs can spike with heavy ML workloads. Pre-purchased credits for discounts.

whereWeWin.headline: Advanced Analytics & ML Beyond SQL
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS provides 200+ algorithms, visual model building, champion/challenger, and production ML governance. Snowflake ML is emerging but limited — Snowpark requires Python coding, and Cortex AI is focused on GenAI/LLM, not traditional ML production workflows.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS has 50 years of validated statistical and ML algorithms. Snowflake ML launched in 2023 — still maturing for enterprise production use.

whereWeWin.headline: Industry Solutions (Fraud, AML, Risk)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS delivers production-ready regulated solutions with compliance built in. Snowflake is a data platform — it stores and processes data but doesn't provide industry-specific analytics solutions.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS Fraud Management detects fraud in real-time with network analytics. Snowflake can store the transaction data but can't detect fraud patterns independently.

whereWeWin.headline: Model Governance for Regulators
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Model Manager provides formal model lifecycle with BSP-grade audit trails. Snowflake's model registry is basic — designed for tracking, not regulated governance.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: BSP Circular 1112 requires formal model governance. SAS meets this natively; Snowflake would need external tools to achieve equivalent governance.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Snowflake is best-in-class for cloud data warehousing — scalability, concurrency, and ease of use. Hard to displace once organizations centralize data there.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Don't compete on data warehousing. Position SAS as the analytics and governance layer that sits ON TOP of Snowflake data. SAS connects natively to Snowflake.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Snowflake's data sharing capabilities are unique — share live data across organizations without copying. Marketplace for third-party data sources.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Acknowledge this is genuinely differentiated. Position SAS as what you DO with the shared data — analytics, governance, decisioning. Data sharing is infrastructure; analytics is the value.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Many analytics teams are SQL-first. Snowflake lets them stay in SQL while adding some ML capabilities via Snowpark ML and ML Functions.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SQL is great for reporting and simple analytics. For production ML, real-time decisioning, and regulatory compliance, you need purpose-built tools. SAS serves both SQL users (via Viya Data Studio) and advanced analytics needs.

features.notes: Snowflake is purpose-built; SAS is not a data warehouse
Snowflake model registry is basic; SAS has regulated governance
Snowflake is a data platform, not a solutions platform
Snowflake is batch/near-real-time; not sub-second decisioning
Snowpark ML is growing but limited algorithm library
Snowsight is basic BI; not comparable to SAS VA
Unique Snowflake capability — live data sharing without copying
Snowflake excels at concurrent SQL workloads
Cortex AI brings LLMs into Snowflake natively
SAS has dedicated enterprise forecasting; Snowflake has basic ML functions
Snowflake is cloud-only; SAS supports on-prem

landmines.question: How would you build real-time fraud scoring with sub-10ms latency on Snowflake?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Snowflake is near-real-time at best — not designed for sub-second decisioning
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Intelligent Decisioning scores in <10ms combining rules + ML + network analytics. That's what BSP Circular 1213 requires for real-time fraud monitoring.

landmines.question: If BSP asks to see your model governance workflow — approval chains, documentation, audit trail — can Snowflake provide that?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Basic model registry — no formal governance workflows
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Model Manager provides the formal governance that regulators expect. It connects to Snowflake for data but provides the governance layer on top.

landmines.question: What pre-built fraud detection models does Snowflake offer out of the box?
landmines.expectedAnswer: None — Snowflake is a data platform, not a solutions platform
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Fraud Management has pre-built detection models including Philippine payment fraud patterns. Snowflake stores the data; SAS detects the fraud.

objections.objection: Snowflake can do ML now with Snowpark
objections.whyTheySayIt: Snowpark ML and ML Functions are new. Customers assume Snowflake handles everything.
objections.response: Snowpark ML is promising for simple models on data already in Snowflake. For enterprise needs — 200+ algorithms, network analytics, champion/challenger, regulatory governance, real-time scoring — you need a purpose-built platform. SAS connects to Snowflake for data and adds the analytics and governance layer that Snowpark can't provide.
proofPoints: No BSP/IC regulatory compliance in Snowpark ML
Real-time scoring not possible in Snowflake architecture
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss Snowpark entirely — acknowledge it works for simple ML. Focus on the enterprise/regulated gap.

objections.objection: We already store all our data in Snowflake — why add another platform?
objections.whyTheySayIt: Data consolidation feels efficient. Adding platforms adds complexity.
objections.response: Snowflake is excellent for data storage and SQL analytics. SAS doesn't replace Snowflake — it connects TO Snowflake. Think of it like this: Snowflake is your data foundation, SAS is your analytics and governance engine. You wouldn't use Snowflake for real-time fraud scoring or regulatory model management, just as you wouldn't use SAS as a data warehouse.
proofPoints: SAS has native Snowflake connector — reads data directly
Complementary positioning: data platform + analytics platform
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't position as either/or. SAS + Snowflake is a common enterprise architecture.

phContext.localPresence: Limited direct PH presence. ASEAN team based in Singapore. Growing interest from PH enterprises evaluating cloud data warehouse modernization. Available on AWS/Azure marketplaces.
knownCustomers: Growing interest from banks doing data warehouse modernization
No known large-scale regulated deployments in PH
phContext.localPricing: Consumption-based credits. Competitive with BigQuery/Synapse. Can be attractive for variable workloads. Credits purchased in advance for discounts.
regulatoryGaps: Cloud-only — no on-premises option for data sovereignty
Model governance doesn't meet BSP examination standards

partnerWhen: Customer has Snowflake as data warehouse — SAS connects for analytics
Data team uses Snowflake for data engineering, SAS for production ML and governance
Snowflake sales team is not positioning ML as SAS replacement
Customer needs both data platform and analytics platform
competeWhen: Snowflake positioning Snowpark ML as complete ML platform
Customer evaluating Snowflake for analytics (not just data warehousing)
Primary need is advanced analytics/real-time decisioning (not data warehousing)

proofPoints.title: Complementary market positioning
proofPoints.detail: Gartner positions Snowflake as Cloud Database Management Systems leader; SAS as Advanced Analytics and AI leader. Different categories — complementary architecture.

proofPoints.title: SAS + Snowflake enterprise architecture
proofPoints.detail: Multiple global enterprises use Snowflake as data warehouse + SAS for analytics and governance — standard enterprise pattern.

proofPoints.title: Real-time scoring architecture
proofPoints.detail: Snowflake's architecture is not designed for sub-second scoring. SAS Intelligent Decisioning provides <10ms latency for real-time fraud detection.
